decision: Slow-mo powerup drives Rapier via manual stepping: <Physics paused> + a single PhysicsStepDriver useFrame(priority -10) calling rapier.step(realDt * timeScale()).
why: @react-three/rapier's fixed-step accumulator advances sim-time 1:1 with real-time regardless of the timeStep prop (cadence threshold == integration dt, ratio fixed at 1; stepWorld overwrites world.timestep each step so external override is futile). The exposed useRapier().step(dt) is the SAME function the auto-loop uses (accumulator, event queue, sensors, interpolation all intact), so passing a scaled dt is genuine bullet-time with no behavioral loss.

decision: Score-doubler powerup doubles CRYSTAL value while active (scoreMultiplier() in powerupBridge, applied at CrystalField collect), not a final-score multiplier.
why: computeScore is a pure function of run TOTALS (height monotonic, crystals discrete, maxCombo a max) computed at run end — there is no running score counter to multiply during a window. Crystals are the only discrete, collectible score source that maps cleanly to a timed multiplier, so the doubler doubles each gem's value for its duration.

decision: Multi-bounce powerup is a CHARGE STACK (bounceCharges count, not a timer): grants 3 free mid-air bounces, each spent by an airborne TAP (useDrag tap flag) → requestMidAirBounce → PlayerBlob pops the blob to +22 vy. Pickups stack additively.
why: A timed mid-air-bounce buff is awkward (you'd waste it if no bounce was needed in the window); a discrete charge stack lets the player save the recovery for when they actually mis-arc. Tap (vs drag) cleanly separates it from the existing mid-air steering drag with no new input surface.
